checks.a_analysis: The output content accurately reflects the headings, lists, and paragraphs found in the source document. No fabricated sections, introductions, or body text were identified. All headings such as 'Framework+ Datasets', 'Working Groups', and 'Regular Status Updates' directly correspond to the source PDF.

The output contains the URL 'www.nconemap.gov/pages/metadata' which is present in the source document under the 'Metadata Committee' section. No other URLs, phone numbers, or addresses are present in the output that were not in the source. The text 'Join meeting' is also present as a placeholder for a web conference link, matching the source text.

All main sections of the agenda (Welcome, Framework+ Datasets, Working Groups, Meetings, Status Updates, Other Business, Action Items, Adjourn) are present in the output. The single-page source document is fully captured.
The document does not contain formal tables. The '2021 Meetings' section, which is presented as a list of dates in the source, is correctly captured as a paragraph containing all three dates.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: The output successfully preserves the content of the agenda items, including nested bullets and names of presenters. A minor omission of the footer text ('SMAC Web Site... NC OneMap Web Site...') was noted; however, this single line represents less than 5% of the total document text, which is well below the 25% threshold for a major defect according to the provided instructions (where losing a single sentence from a long section is acceptable).

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text content in the output maintains the meaning and terminology of the source. Dates, names, and technical terms (e.g., 'Orthoimagery', 'Cadastral', 'NextGen 911') are accurately transcribed. Formatting changes such as italics or nested lists are within acceptable conversion parameters.

checks.a_analysis: The content appears correctly without duplication. Each heading, list item, and paragraph in the output corresponds to exactly one instance in the source PDF. The title and 'Agenda' text are mapped once to metadata/section titles and headings without being repeated in the subsequent elements.

The heading hierarchy is logical and consistently uses level 2 for major sections and level 3 for the sidebar section ('2021 Meetings'). While no explicit H1 is present in the elements array, the document title is correctly promoted to the section title, which is an acceptable normalization for screen reader navigation.

The source document does not contain any formal tables. The '2021 Meetings' section, which is visually aligned to the right, has been correctly interpreted as a separate heading and paragraph block, preserving its informational structure.

The reading order in the JSON follows the logical flow of the agenda. It handles the sidebar '2021 Meetings' by placing it after the 'Working Groups' list, which is the most accessible linear representation of the source's visual layout. One minor omission is the footer URLs, which is addressed in S7.

The list structures are captured with high fidelity. Nested lists, such as the three-level hierarchy under 'NC Board on Geographic Names' (Board -> Discussion -> Mountain names), are correctly identified and nested within the JSON structure.

The pipeline failed to capture several links and URLs as interactive or specifically styled elements. The text 'minutes' (under Welcome) and 'Join meeting' (under Web Conference) are interactive links in the source but are rendered as plain text (or italicized text) without link metadata in the output. Furthermore, the URLs present in the footer of the source document ('http://it.nc.gov/gicc' and 'www.nconemap.gov') are entirely missing from the output elements.
checks.b_evidence_source:
SMAC Web Site – http://it.nc.gov/gicc | NC OneMap Web Site - www.nconemap.gov (footer URLs)
Approval of October 14, 2020 minutes (minutes is an underlined link)
checks.b_evidence_output: The text 'minutes' has 'underline': false and no link reference. The footer URLs are absent from the elements array.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections[0].elements, Welcome/Introductions section and document footer

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output successfully translates the PDF's layout into a logical web structure. The '2021 Meetings' block, which was floated to the right in the PDF, is now placed in the main content flow below the 'Working Groups' section. This is an appropriate responsive design choice that maintains the visual organization and does not hinder readability or comprehension.

The source document does not contain bordered tables. The listing of dates under '2021 Meetings' is clearly rendered as text in the HTML output. Although the layout changed from a vertical list to a horizontal line, the data remains perfectly readable and structured.
The source PDF contains no meaningful images, logos, or diagrams. Only text and decorative lines are present. No images are missing in the HTML rendering.

There is no duplication of body content. The document title appearing at the top of the page and again at the start of the main body content is an intentional and expected accessibility feature for navigation landmarks.
